description: Verso: Narrative in Judaeo-Arabic. Perhaps notes for a sermon. It starts by describing a stroll on the Sabbath following Pentecost to 'the observatory' (al-raṣad) to get some fresh air (natanashshaq). One of the members of the party, Elʿazar b. Shelomo asked the writer "What is the mountain of God?" The writer then expounds on the nature of the mountain of God. Dating: A previous PGP description listed this as 11th century, but it is not clear on what basis. (Information in part from Goitein's index cards.) ASE
description_he: טקסט פרא-ספרותי ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
